usage: Original Caledonian Pine Maximum reconstruction, retained for comparison
note: Generated for this project; represents an Atlantic pinewood grove, not a historical photograph.

usage: Preferred Caledonian Pine Maximum timeline background; brighter and more open on the left
note: Edited from the first generated pinewood image to preserve the right-side Scots pine, birch and rowan while reducing left-side darkness and crowding.

usage: Previous Caledonian Pine Maximum timeline background with added left-side birch and common juniper
note: Edited from v2 to add downy/silver birch and common juniper to the open left-side pine-heath mosaic. Birch is strongly plausible from Betula evidence; species-level separation should be treated cautiously.

usage: Preferred Caledonian Pine Maximum timeline background with mature and young Scots pines added among the left-side birches
note: Edited from v3 to add one mature Scots pine and scattered young Scots pines to the left-side pine-birch-heath mosaic while keeping the scene open.
Generated because no photograph exists for this period.

usage: Early Holocene birch and hazel woodland timeline background
note: Generated because no photograph exists for this early Holocene habitat state.

usage: Mid-Holocene mixed broadleaf wildwood timeline background
note: Generated as a historical reconstruction; Killarney photos are retained as modern references.

usage: Blanket bog expansion and woodland opening timeline background
note: Generated to avoid implying modern bog photos are direct historical records.

usage: Present-day garden rewilding timeline background and rewilding section
note: Generated visualisation of a plausible native garden habitat.

usage: Interactive Rewilding section: native layers scene
note: Shows a plausible layered Irish garden habitat with native trees, shrubs, long grass and leaf litter.

usage: Interactive Rewilding section: fish-free wildlife pond scene
note: Shows pond features and likely pond wildlife such as frogs and damselflies.

usage: Interactive Rewilding section: deadwood and decay scene
note: Shows deadwood, leaf litter, fungi and shaded cover as habitat for decomposers and sheltering wildlife.

usage: Interactive Rewilding section: pollinator meadow and hedge corridor scene
note: Shows less-mown flowering grassland, native hedge structure and wildlife movement habitat.
